name: project-to-obsidian
description:
将代码项目转换为 Obsidian 知识库。当用户提到 obsidian、项目文档、知识库、分析项目、转换项目 时激活。

【激活后必须执行】：
1. 先完整阅读本 SKILL.md 文件
2. 理解 AI 写入规则（默认到 00_Inbox/AI/、追加式、统一 Schema）
3. 执行 STEP 0: 使用 AskUserQuestion 询问用户确认
4. 用户确认后才开始 STEP 1 项目扫描
5. 严格按 STEP 0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 顺序执行

【禁止行为】：
- 禁止不读 SKILL.md 就开始分析项目
- 禁止跳过 STEP 0 用户确认
- 禁止直接在 30_Resources 创建（先到 00_Inbox/AI/）
- 禁止自作主张决定输出位置
capabilities: Analyze project structure and identify project type
Extract functions, classes, and API endpoints from code
Create bidirectional links and tags for knowledge graph
Support multiple programming languages and frameworks
author: Claude Code
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate documentation generator that converts code projects to Obsidian knowledge bases. It requires user confirmation before execution, scans project files for documentation purposes only, and generates structured markdown templates. No malicious intent or harmful behavior detected.
valueStatement: Transform any code project into a searchable, linkable Obsidian knowledge base. The skill analyzes project structure, code, and APIs to automatically generate structured documentation.
userTitle: Convert Code to Obsidian Knowledge Base
limitations: Requires MCP integration for direct Obsidian vault writing
May not handle very large monorepos efficiently
Limited to supported languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Ruby, PHP
Output quality depends on code comments and structure

useCases.targetUser: Software developers who need project documentation
useCases.title: Developer Documentation
useCases.description: Automatically generate comprehensive documentation for your codebase including API references, module descriptions, and architecture diagrams.

useCases.targetUser: Technical leads and architects managing multiple projects
useCases.title: Knowledge Management
useCases.description: Convert legacy projects into searchable knowledge bases that can be linked with other notes and explored via bidirectional links.

useCases.title: Onboarding Support
useCases.description: Create structured documentation to help new team members understand project structure, APIs, and setup procedures.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Project Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the project-to-obsidian skill to generate a quick overview of my project at [PATH]. Give me the project structure and main components.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick understanding of an unfamiliar codebase

promptTemplates.title: Full Documentation Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /p2o [PATH] to convert my project to an Obsidian knowledge base. Generate complete documentation including architecture, API docs, and module descriptions.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need comprehensive project documentation

promptTemplates.title: API Documentation Only
promptTemplates.prompt: Use project-to-obsidian to generate API documentation for [PATH]. Focus on endpoints, request/response formats, and function signatures.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to document APIs for external consumers

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /p2o [PATH] --arch to analyze and document the architecture of my project. Include system design, dependencies, and component relationships.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to understand or document system architecture

bestPractices: Always confirm output location with user before writing files
Use the recommended 00_Inbox/AI/ directory for initial output
Run with user confirmation step enabled to prevent accidental writes
Choose the appropriate generation mode (full, quick, API, or architecture)
antiPatterns: Do not skip the user confirmation step (STEP 0)
Do not write directly to 30_Resources without going through Inbox first
Do not analyze projects without reading the SKILL.md first
Do not overwrite existing notes - use append mode

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Ruby, and PHP. It detects project type via config files like package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, etc.

faq.question: How does it write to Obsidian?
faq.answer: The skill can write directly to Obsidian vault using MCP tools (obsidian_append_content), create local folders, or output to project /docs directory.

faq.question: Does it read my .env files?
faq.answer: The skill scans for .env* patterns to detect configuration files but does NOT read or exfiltrate sensitive environment variables.

faq.question: Can I customize the output structure?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses customizable templates for different document types. You can modify the templates in the templates/ directory.

faq.question: What if my project is very large?
faq.answer: For large projects, use the quick mode (--quick) to generate just the overview and structure first, then generate specific sections as needed.

faq.question: Is this safe to use with production code?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill only reads and analyzes your code locally. It does not make network requests or execute arbitrary commands. User confirmation is required before any file writing.
